disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden severe breathlessness that woke the patient from sleep about 90 minutes ago and forces him to sit bolt upright to breathe
trigger_question_examples: When did the breathing trouble start?
Can you breathe any easier in any position?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He woke up out of a dead sleep about an hour and a half ago gasping. He can only get a bit of air if he sits dead straight up on the edge of the bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cough productive of frothy, pink, blood-tinged fluid over the last 30 minutes
trigger_question_examples: What does the phlegm look like?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: In the last half hour he's started coughing up this frothy pink stuff — it looks like it's got a bit of blood in it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms dramatically worsen on lying flat and are partially relieved only by sitting bolt upright (orthopnoea / paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea pattern)
trigger_question_examples: Does lying down make it worse?
Do you ever wake in the night short of breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: If he tries to lie back at all he feels like he's going under — he has to stay bolt upright. This is what woke him.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cold, sweaty, clammy, pale skin — patient exhausted by the effort of breathing
trigger_question_examples: How does his skin look and feel?
Is he sweaty or a strange colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's gone pale and he's cold and drenched in sweat, and he's absolutely worn out from trying to breathe.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chest tightness and an overwhelming sense of impending doom
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any chest pain or tightness?
Do you feel frightened or like something bad is about to happen?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His chest feels tight and he keeps saying he feels like something terrible is about to happen.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Missed the regular daily water tablet (furosemide) yesterday; takes a blood-pressure tablet (ramipril); has a CPAP machine for sleep apnoea that was not worn tonight
trigger_question_examples: What medications does he take, and did he take them as usual?
Does he use anything at night for his breathing, like a CPAP machine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He takes a water tablet every day but he forgot it yesterday, and a blood-pressure tablet. He's got a CPAP machine for sleep apnoea but he didn't put it on tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Both lower legs are puffy and swollen up to about mid-calf
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any swelling in the legs or ankles?
Are his ankles or legs more puffy than usual?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, both his legs have been puffy and swollen — up to about halfway to the knee.
